family_intent: Canonical-path antibody wave — align test-currency for selection-family materialization, sparse-monitor vig handling, and canonical event shape under the post-T4.1b canonical write path.

t2_slices_closed.scope: D5 sparse-monitor imputes missing sibling prices
t2_slices_closed.outcome: closed full — T6.3 Option C flipped the xfail(strict=True) marker via XPASS detection; fixture asymmetrized to [0.20,0.50,0.30] to kill silent-sibling-equivalence ambiguity (con-nyx pre-edit finding a); asymmetry assertion added so p_cal impute source is discriminable from symmetric-sibling outcomes.
t2_slices_closed.closure_trigger_fired: T6.3 VigTreatment.from_raw sparse-impute implementation 2026-04-24 (Option C per architectural decision)

t2_slices_closed.scope: SelectionFamilySubstrate 3 tests rewritten against canonical post-T4.1b event shape

t2_slices_closed.test: new test exercises evaluate_candidate with Day0Router.route NOT stubbed; Day0HighSignal constructs and computes p_vector over fixture bins
t2_slices_closed.current_blocker: Day0HighSignal.p_vector requires a populated Day0TemporalContext (solar_day, clock_semantics, etc.) that the fixture's SimpleNamespace stub does not carry
t2_slices_closed.rationale: Plan estimated size 3h for full real-Day0Router integration fixture. Rather than block T2 closure on building that fixture, this slice lands the xfail antibody so coverage is visible and a future fixture-build slice removes the marker. strict=False allows the test to pass silently if the xfail is removed (signal of fixture progress) rather than failing loudly.

unresolved_deferrals: T2.g xfail clears when Day0TemporalContext fixture builder lands (separate slice, ~3h)

closure_history.T2.c: 2026-04-24 — T6.3 Option C landed; xfail(strict=True) XPASS forced marker removal; fixture asymmetrized per con-nyx pre-edit finding a.

t2_d_1_substitute_slice.original_spec: v2 snapshot fixture setup to populate ensemble_snapshots_v2 with boundary_ambiguous=0 row
t2_d_1_substitute_slice.delivered_instead: surrogate-critic-driven verification that init_schema(conn) already creates empty ensemble_snapshots_v2; natural empty-table query returns {} so DT7 gate passes without stubbing. F1 HIGH finding from adversarial critic prompt caught that an explicit monkeypatch was redundant and would have cemented bad precedent. TODO(T2.g) breadcrumbs added at top of each of 3 T2.d/e/f tests so future real-fixture slice has explicit anchors.

critic_prompt_degradation_fix.context: Operator flagged 6+ prior slices producing zero substantive surrogate findings. Root cause: prompts had accreted self-justification language ("pattern proven", "narrow scope, self-validating") that primed APPROVE.
critic_prompt_degradation_fix.fix: Rewrote surrogate dispatch prompt for T2.d.1 with 10 explicit adversarial asks, no scope-narrowing language, specific failure-mode probes.
critic_prompt_degradation_fix.result: Surrogate returned REQUEST_CHANGES with 6 findings (HIGH×1 + MED×2 + LOW×3). F1 HIGH caught real redundant-stub issue that would have cemented bad precedent. All 6 findings addressed inline.
critic_prompt_degradation_fix.lesson: Adversarial framing is load-bearing for critic machinery. Future slices must preserve it; never skip surrogate on 'narrow scope' grounds without active challenge.

cross_references.paired_t6_3_slice: plan L103 — T6.3 VigTreatment.from_raw implementation (size 10h, deps T2.c boundary test)
